status: active policy; helper module required for terrain-aware road replacement
goal: Roads should connect settlements and camps without carving nonsense paths over rivers, cliffs, or steep terrain.
installed_bridge_layers: YUNG's Bridges generates standalone world bridges.
Macaw's Bridges supplies buildable medieval bridge blocks.
Macaw's Lights and Lamps supplies wall lanterns for custom guild structures.

rules.rule: When an authored road crosses water or a ravine, prefer a bridge structure/piece instead of a flat path.
rules.current_owner: manual structure templates and future helper module

rules.rule: Roads should not float or shear through cliffs. Use stair/bridge/support pieces when elevation changes sharply.
rules.current_owner: manual structure templates and future helper module

rules.rule: Road/bridge materials should follow region identity: spruce/cobble north, sandstone/acacia south, jungle/mangrove east, stone/dark-oak west.
Village-connected roads should respect the widened village structure spacing and should not force multiple full village systems into one footprint.
rules.current_owner: Sparse Structures, Towns and Towers, Integrated Villages, Ascendant Guild spacing

implementation_boundary: Datapacks can define bridge structures and village pieces.
Existing mods can generate their own bridges and roads.
A helper module is required to inspect generated terrain, detect a road/ravine/river crossing, and substitute Macaw/YUNG-style bridge pieces dynamically.
fresh_world_checks: Roads should not visibly float over water or cliffs near spawn villages.
YUNG's Bridges should still generate natural bridge landmarks.
Custom guild camp lanterns should use wall lantern variants when attached to walls.
If a road crosses a river without a bridge, record coordinates for helper-module route logic.
